diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaundice reflects conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia from hepatocellular injury and cholestasis; video-observable and patient-reportable in telehealth.
Jaundice with fever is also the cholangitis picture — cannot be distinguished from viral hepatitis without bloods and imaging.
Bilirubinuria from conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia; a reliable patient-reportable marker of cholestatic/hepatocellular jaundice.
Reduced enteral bilirubin (cholestasis) produces acholic stools; supports a cholestatic hepatitic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt febrile prodrome with drenching fatigue and malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hyperacute high-fever prodrome preceding jaundice is characteristic of HAV relative to the more insidious HBV/HCV.
Constitutional gastrointestinal upset is typical of the viral prodrome and drives the dehydration risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent travel to an endemic region with street food and tap water exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Faecal-oral exposure in an endemic area within the HAV incubation window is the key epidemiological link.

symptoms.symptom_name: New strong aversion to cigarettes in a habitual smoker

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A classic (if soft) associated feature of the viral hepatitis prodrome; non-specific but corroborative.
Cholestatic itch from bile-salt deposition; supports a cholestatic component but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender smooth hepatomegaly ~2 cm below the costal margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender hepatomegaly reflects acute hepatic inflammation — a clinician palpation finding that CANNOT be obtained via telehealth (sealed).

symptoms.symptom_name: No asterixis / no clinical hepatic encephalopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of asterixis and preserved cognition argue against established encephalopathy, but formal exclusion needs examination and bloods (INR).

symptoms.symptom_name: No splenomegaly, spider naevi or palmar erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chronic stigmata supports an acute rather than chronic liver process — a clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured low-grade fever (Temp 38.1) with mild tachycardia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Measured fever with jaundice keeps biliary sepsis on the table; the exact temperature is a sealed clinician measurement, not available via chat.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Hepatitis A serology (anti-HAV IgM) plus viral hepatitis screen (HBsAg, anti-HCV)
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive anti-HAV IgM (pending) confirming acute hepatitis A
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Aetiology unconfirmed and other viral hepatitides / drug injury not excluded; notification and contact management delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive anti-HAV IgM is confirmatory for acute HAV; unobtainable via telehealth and must not be assumed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Liver function tests and coagulation profile (bilirubin, ALT/AST, INR)

investigation_details.expected_result: Hepatocellular LFT derangement; INR is the key marker of synthetic function to exclude fulminant failure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fulminant hepatic failure (coagulopathy) cannot be excluded — a potentially fatal miss.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: INR/coagulation is essential to exclude synthetic failure; no raw numeric value is available or should be assumed via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Jaundice + dark urine + pale stools + febrile prodrome + endemic-region faecal-oral exposure is a strongly suggestive travel-acquired viral hepatitis picture — but recognition triggers urgent bloods, not a remote diagnosis.
Fever with new jaundice mandates exclusion of ascending cholangitis with bloods and imaging — the can't-miss surgical/endoscopic emergency behind this presentation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Liver function tests and coagulation profile (INR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies hepatocellular injury and, via INR, excludes fulminant liver failure — the pivotal safety investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral hepatitis serology (anti-HAV IgM, HBsAg, anti-HCV)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms aetiology, distinguishes HAV from HBV/HCV, and enables public-health notification and contact management.
Excludes biliary obstruction / cholangitis in a febrile jaundiced patient — imaging the AI cannot obtain remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine (hydration / renal function)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses dehydration and acute kidney injury from vomiting and poor intake in an isolated patient.
